commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is real behavior proof or maintainer acceptance of the proof gap, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to make.; Cleared: The diff only changes Feishu runtime filtering and colocated tests; it does not touch workflows, lockfiles, dependency sources, scripts, package metadata, permissions, or secret handling. (sha=d5d2ae2307ff4ea7749dfbb16fb4e1626c86993a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Feishu receive-event self-open-id filter before processing claims/debounce and adds monitor message-handler regression tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +149. Total +158 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main parses im.message.receive_v1 events and claims/enqueues them before any bot-open-id self filter. I did not run a live Feishu WebSocket incident reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is real behavior proof or maintainer acceptance of the proof gap, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to make.

target.title: fix(feishu): drop self-authored receive echoes
target.body:
## Summary

- Drop Feishu receive events authored by the current bot open_id before the in-flight processing claim and inbound debounce queue.
- Add focused monitor-message-handler regressions for self echoes, ordinary users, and other app-authored senders.

Fixes #90559.

## Root cause

Feishu `im.message.receive_v1` events enter `createFeishuMessageReceiveHandler`, then immediately claim processing and enqueue into the inbound debouncer. Current `main`, `v2026.6.1`, and `v2026.6.4-alpha.1` have no early self-message filter on that path, so a Feishu echo of the bot's own send can be treated as fresh inbound input and start another agent turn.

The repair is intentionally narrow: it only drops events whose sender `open_id` matches the stored bot open_id for the account. It does not drop all `sender_type: app` messages, so other app-authored messages can still flow through normal Feishu policy and dispatch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu bot-send echoes can no longer consume processing/debounce capacity or dispatch another agent turn when the receive event sender open_id matches the configured bot open_id.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS using the repo Vitest wrapper; no live Feishu WebSocket credentials were available in this session.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/monitor.message-handler.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/sequential-queue.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.webhook-e2e.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: 5 test files passed, 115 tests passed. The new regression proves a bot-open-id echo returns before debounce/dispatch, a same-message-id user retry still dispatches, a different app-authored sender still dispatches, and ordinary user messages still dispatch.

Observed result after fix: The self-authored receive event logs `dropping self-authored message ...` and does not call the debouncer or `handleFeishuMessage`; non-self events still reach dispatch.

What was not tested: No live Feishu WebSocket slow-LLM incident reproduction was run. Queue abort, backpressure, and configurable timeout policy are broader follow-up decisions and are not changed here.

## Verification

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/feishu/src/monitor.message-handler.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.message-handler.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/monitor.message-handler.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/sequential-queue.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.webhook-e2e.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` after narrowing the filter to bot-open-id matches only; final result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Evidence checked

- Feishu receive/message sender contract: official Feishu docs document `im.message.receive_v1` sender metadata and message sender `sender_type`/id fields.
- Local dependency contract: `@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk@1.66.0` types expose `im.message.receive_v1.sender.sender_id.open_id` and `sender_type`.
- Current/shipped behavior: `origin/main`, `v2026.6.1`, and `v2026.6.4-alpha.1` route receive events into `tryBeginFeishuMessageProcessing` and `inboundDebouncer.enqueue` without an early self-filter.
target.author: baskduf
